persons.arguments_against: Counter-hypothesis: Dutton is common (21 staff officials, 13 biographies), the bio is a two-initial 'T. E.' form, and neither the honour nor edition-cooccurrence bonus fires, so name/initials are weak. This fails because the bio supplies an idiosyncratic detail that the staff list confirms exactly: 'traffic supt., Ceylon, 2nd Oct., 1913; designation altered to traffic man., 1916' -- the staff list shows the SAME post renamed from Traffic Superintendent (1915) to Traffic Manager (1917), not a new role. The career then ends at the singular head office, General Manager of the Ceylon Government Railways (bio 'gen. man., Ceylon Govt. Rlys., July, 1923' / staff General Manager from 1925). A renamed single post plus the singular General Managership leaves no room for a second T. E. Dutton on this exact railway track.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second T. E. Dutton in the Ceylon Railway Department over 1915-1931, or that the General Manager from 1925 was a different man than the bio's railway officer.

notes: The bio's distinctive note that the Traffic Superintendent designation was 'altered to traffic man., 1916' matches the staff list's Traffic Superintendent (1915) -> Traffic Manager (1917) rename, and the career terminates in the singular General Managership of the Ceylon Government Railways. Common surname is offset by this specific, dated, single-office trajectory.
